title: Little barista treasure hunt
summary: A playful kids activity with simple cafe clues, color tasks, and a small reward at the end.
body: Little barista treasure hunt helps children explore Wake Cup through safe and simple tasks connected to colors, pastries, cups, and table details.
The activity works well during family cafe visits, birthdays, or quiet daytime hours when children need a small task.
Each step should be short and easy to understand, with adults or staff guiding the process when needed.
steps: Find three colors on the menu
Spot the hidden coffee cup symbol
highlights: Good for birthdays and family visits

title: Coffee flavor trail
summary: A light interactive tasting that guides guests through aroma, sweetness, texture, and pastry pairing clues.
body: Coffee flavor trail turns a cafe visit into a compact shared activity.
Guests move through easy prompts connected to espresso, milk drinks, filter coffee, and pastries.
The final stop can lead into a tasting, a small gift, or a booking conversation depending on the format selected for the group.
steps: Finish with a shared coffee moment
highlights: Simple clue path designed for mixed guest groups
Flexible fit for coffee tastings and small gatherings
Finishes with a tasting-led reward moment

title: Family menu mystery
summary: A shared family activity where kids and adults solve menu clues together before coffee or dessert.
body: Family menu mystery is made for groups where children and adults want to play together without leaving the table for too long.
The tasks combine easy kid-friendly clues with small coffee, pastry, and service questions that adults can enjoy too.
It works well before dessert, during birthdays, or as a light entertainment option for family cafe guests.
highlights: Good for families and mixed ages
Works at the table or around approved areas
Easy to connect with dessert or small gifts
